zotero.abstractNote: We propose Slot Attention, a module that maps from features extracted by a convolutional neural network to a fixed set of object slots. The slots interact with each other through a competitive attention mechanism, which encourages them to specialize in explaining different parts of the input. We show that Slot Attention can be used as a component in an autoencoder to perform object-centric representation learning. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results on challenging synthetic datasets and generalizes to complex, real-world images, where it can be used to perform object-centric reasoning and manipulation.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 33 (NeurIPS 2020)

extra.Editors: H. Larochelle and M. Ranzato and R. Hadsell and M. F. Balcan and H. Lin
